nodes.name: Value Semantics & Object Model
nodes.blurb: Objects are values with identity, lifetime, and copies you can reason about.

problems.name: Return a big object by value

nodes.name: References & Const-Correctness
nodes.blurb: Bind without copying and let const document (and enforce) intent.

problems.name: Pass by const-ref vs value benchmark

nodes.name: The Type System: auto & decltype
nodes.blurb: Let the compiler name your types and preserve their exact category.

nodes.name: RAII: Resource Acquisition Is Initialization
nodes.blurb: Tie every resource to an object's lifetime so cleanup is automatic.

nodes.name: Lambdas & Closures
nodes.blurb: Inline function objects that capture state and drive callbacks.

nodes.name: STL Containers & Their Tradeoffs
nodes.blurb: Pick the right container by knowing its memory layout and complexity.

problems.name: Pick the right container (map vs unordered_map)

nodes.name: Overload Resolution & ADL
nodes.blurb: How the compiler chooses one function among many, and where it looks.

nodes.name: The Rule of 0 / 3 / 5
nodes.blurb: Own a resource and you owe five special members — or better, zero.

problems.name: Rule of five for an owning buffer

nodes.name: Exception Safety & Guarantees
nodes.blurb: Write code that stays correct even when an operation throws mid-flight.

problems.name: RAII to avoid leaks on throw

nodes.name: Iterators & Ranges of Data
nodes.blurb: The uniform cursor abstraction that connects containers to algorithms.

problems.name: Range-based for over a custom type

nodes.name: Templates & Argument Deduction
nodes.blurb: Write once, generate for many types, and let deduction fill in the blanks.

problems.name: Class template with a default argument

nodes.name: Move Semantics & Rvalue References
nodes.blurb: Steal resources from expiring objects instead of copying them.

problems.name: std::move a member out of a class

nodes.name: Algorithms & Ranges
nodes.blurb: Replace raw loops with composable, correct standard algorithms.

problems.name: Rewrite a raw loop with std algorithms

nodes.name: constexpr & Compile-Time Computation
nodes.blurb: Push work from run time to compile time for free results and constants.

nodes.name: Undefined Behavior & Strict Aliasing
nodes.blurb: Know the sharp edges the compiler assumes you will never touch.

nodes.name: Ownership & Smart Pointers
nodes.blurb: Model unique, shared, and non-owning ownership with the right pointer.

problems.name: Break a shared_ptr cycle with weak_ptr

nodes.name: Copy Elision & RVO
nodes.blurb: When the compiler builds the result in place and skips the copy entirely.

problems.name: Trigger NRVO and observe no copy

nodes.name: noexcept & the Move-or-Copy Choice
nodes.blurb: Promise not to throw, and unlock faster library fast paths.

nodes.name: Variadic Templates & Fold Expressions
nodes.blurb: Take any number of arguments of any types and forward them perfectly.

nodes.name: SFINAE → Concepts
nodes.blurb: Constrain templates cleanly with C++20 concepts instead of enable_if tricks.

nodes.name: CRTP & Static Polymorphism
nodes.blurb: Reuse behavior through the derived type with zero virtual overhead.

nodes.name: variant / optional / expected
nodes.blurb: Model 'one of', 'maybe', and 'value or error' as first-class value types.

nodes.name: Hot-Path Code & Bit Tricks
nodes.blurb: Branchless, inlinable, cache-friendly code where every cycle counts.

problems.name: Round up to power of two

nodes.name: Memory Model & std::atomic
nodes.blurb: Reason about visibility and ordering across threads with atomics.

nodes.name: Type Erasure
nodes.blurb: Hide concrete types behind a uniform interface, like std::function does.

nodes.name: Concurrency: Threads, Mutex, CV
nodes.blurb: Coordinate threads safely with mutexes and condition variables.

nodes.name: Coroutines (C++20)
nodes.blurb: Suspend and resume functions to write lazy generators and async tasks.

nodes.name: Lock-Free Data Structures
nodes.blurb: Build wait-free and lock-free queues with atomics and careful ordering.

nodes.name: Encapsulation & Invariants
nodes.blurb: Hide state behind an interface so every object always satisfies its class invariant.

nodes.name: Value vs Reference Semantics
nodes.blurb: Decide whether a type copies like an int or is shared by reference, and design its lifetime accordingly.

nodes.name: Composition over Inheritance
nodes.blurb: Prefer assembling behavior from members (has-a) instead of deep class hierarchies (is-a).

nodes.name: RAII as a Design Tool
nodes.blurb: Tie resource ownership to object lifetime so cleanup is automatic and exception-safe.

nodes.name: Value Objects vs Entities
nodes.blurb: Model things by value when equality is by content, and by identity when they have a lifecycle.

nodes.name: SOLID: SRP & OCP
nodes.blurb: One reason to change per class, and open for extension but closed for modification.

problems.name: Split a god class by responsibility
Add a new type without editing old code

nodes.name: Coupling, Cohesion & Demeter
nodes.blurb: Maximize cohesion within a module, minimize coupling between them, and don't talk to strangers.

nodes.name: SOLID: LSP, ISP & DIP
nodes.blurb: Subtypes must be substitutable, interfaces small and client-specific, and dependencies point at abstractions.

nodes.name: Dependency Injection
nodes.blurb: Pass collaborators in from outside instead of constructing them internally, so wiring lives at the edges.

nodes.name: Singleton
nodes.blurb: Guarantee a single shared instance with controlled access, while knowing why it's often a smell.

nodes.name: Factory, Abstract Factory, Builder & Prototype
nodes.blurb: Encapsulate object construction: factories choose the type, builders assemble step by step, prototypes clone.

nodes.name: Adapter & Facade
nodes.blurb: Wrap an incompatible interface to fit a client, or hide a subsystem behind one simple entry point.

nodes.name: Decorator, Proxy & Flyweight
nodes.blurb: Wrap an object to add behavior, control access to it, or share immutable state across many instances.

nodes.name: Composite
nodes.blurb: Treat individual objects and compositions of objects uniformly through a shared tree interface.

nodes.name: Strategy & Template Method
nodes.blurb: Vary an algorithm by injecting a pluggable strategy, or by overriding steps of a fixed skeleton.

nodes.name: Observer
nodes.blurb: Let subjects notify a changing set of subscribers without knowing who they are.

nodes.name: Command, State & Chain of Responsibility
nodes.blurb: Reify a request as an object to queue, undo, hand along a chain, or drive an explicit state machine.

nodes.name: Visitor & Iterator
nodes.blurb: Add operations over a fixed object structure without touching it, and traverse containers uniformly.

nodes.name: CRTP & Static Polymorphism
nodes.blurb: Use the curiously recurring template pattern for compile-time polymorphism and reusable mixins.

nodes.name: Type Erasure & Sum Types
nodes.blurb: Choose runtime polymorphism via type-erased wrappers versus closed sets modeled with std::variant.

nodes.name: PIMPL, Firewalls & ABI
nodes.blurb: Hide implementation behind a pointer to cut compile-time coupling and keep a stable binary interface.

nodes.name: Error-Handling Architecture
nodes.blurb: Design failure paths deliberately: exceptions, std::expected, or error codes, and where each boundary sits.

nodes.name: Hexagonal / Ports & Adapters
nodes.blurb: Isolate domain logic behind ports and plug infrastructure in through adapters for testable layering.

nodes.name: Data-Oriented Design vs OOP
nodes.blurb: Lay out data for the machine, not the object model: contrast AoS with SoA for cache-friendly performance.

nodes.name: Hashing & Frequency Counting
nodes.blurb: Trade space for time with hash maps/sets to look up, dedupe, and count in O(1); the foundation for most array problems.

nodes.name: Two Pointers
nodes.blurb: Walk two indices toward or away from each other on a sorted or symmetric array to converge on pairs/palindromes in O(n).

problems.name: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted

nodes.name: Prefix Sum
nodes.blurb: Precompute running totals so any range/subarray sum answers in O(1); pair with a hash map to count subarrays hitting a target.

nodes.name: Binary Search
nodes.blurb: Halve a sorted (or monotonic) search space each step; also 'binary search the answer' when you can test a candidate in O(n).

problems.name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array

nodes.name: Stack
nodes.blurb: Use a LIFO stack to match nested structure, defer work, and evaluate expressions in one pass.

nodes.name: Linked List
nodes.blurb: Manipulate nodes by rewiring next-pointers; master reversal, merging, and dummy-head bookkeeping.

problems.name: Remove Nth Node From End of List

nodes.name: Bit Manipulation
nodes.blurb: Use XOR, masks, and shifts to pack state, cancel pairs, and compute without extra memory.

nodes.name: Sliding Window
nodes.blurb: Grow and shrink a contiguous window to answer 'best/longest/shortest subarray or substring' problems in O(n).

problems.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

nodes.name: Monotonic Stack
nodes.blurb: Keep the stack sorted while scanning to find the next/previous greater or smaller element in amortized O(n).

nodes.name: Fast & Slow Pointers
nodes.blurb: Run two pointers at different speeds (Floyd's tortoise-and-hare) to detect cycles and find midpoints without extra space.

nodes.name: Trees: BFS & DFS
nodes.blurb: Traverse binary trees recursively (DFS) or level-by-level (BFS) to compute depth, structure, and aggregates.

problems.name: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree

nodes.name: Heap / Priority Queue
nodes.blurb: Keep the top-K or the running min/max cheaply with a binary heap; ideal for streaming and scheduling problems.

problems.name: Kth Largest Element in an Array

nodes.name: Intervals
nodes.blurb: Sort by start/end, then sweep to merge overlaps, insert, or count conflicts.

nodes.name: Matrix Manipulation
nodes.blurb: Treat a 2-D grid as an array with (row, col) math to rotate, spiral, or mark in place.

nodes.name: Binary Search Tree
nodes.blurb: Exploit the left<root<right invariant so search, insert, and ordered traversal run in O(height).

problems.name: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
Kth Smallest Element in a BST
Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree
Insert into a Binary Search Tree

nodes.name: Backtracking
nodes.blurb: Explore the decision tree with DFS, choosing then un-choosing to enumerate valid configurations while pruning dead ends.

problems.name: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number

nodes.name: Graphs: BFS/DFS & Islands
nodes.blurb: Model connectivity as nodes and edges, then flood-fill or traverse grids/adjacency lists to count components and shortest hops.

nodes.name: Greedy
nodes.blurb: Make the locally optimal choice at each step and prove it yields the global optimum; watch for reachability and exchange arguments.

nodes.name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
nodes.blurb: Store strings in a character-branched tree for O(L) prefix lookups; the backbone of autocomplete and word-search.

problems.name: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

nodes.name: Subsets & Permutations
nodes.blurb: Systematically generate every subset, combination, or ordering via backtracking, handling duplicates with sorting/skips.

nodes.name: Topological Sort
nodes.blurb: Order a DAG's nodes so every edge points forward using Kahn's BFS or DFS post-order; detects cycles in dependency problems.

nodes.name: Union-Find (DSU)
nodes.blurb: Maintain disjoint sets with union-by-rank and path compression for near-O(1) connectivity queries and cycle detection.

problems.name: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph

nodes.name: 1-D Dynamic Programming
nodes.blurb: Turn overlapping subproblems into a linear DP table/recurrence; memoize or tabulate over a single dimension.

nodes.name: 2-D Dynamic Programming
nodes.blurb: Extend DP to a grid/table indexed by two variables to solve matching, pathing, and knapsack-style problems.

nodes.name: What Is an FPGA
nodes.blurb: A field-programmable gate array is a chip of reconfigurable logic (LUTs, flip-flops, and CLBs) you wire into any digital circuit after manufacturing.

problems.name: Implement a 2-to-1 mux in one LUT
Map a 4-input AND to a LUT4

nodes.name: Hard Blocks: BRAM & DSP
nodes.blurb: Beyond the soft fabric sit dedicated block RAM and DSP slices that give you cheap on-chip memory and fast multiply-accumulate.

nodes.name: FPGA vs ASIC vs CPU
nodes.blurb: FPGAs trade the raw speed of an ASIC and the flexibility of a CPU for reconfigurable, spatial, parallel compute.

problems.name: Compare latency of a filter on CPU vs FPGA
Decide FPGA or ASIC for a fixed-volume product

nodes.name: Hardware Thinking: Parallel by Default
nodes.blurb: HDL describes structure, not a sequence: everything you write runs at once unless you deliberately serialize it.

nodes.name: Combinational Logic
nodes.blurb: Purely combinational logic maps inputs to outputs with no memory, built from gates, muxes, and lookup tables.

nodes.name: Toolchain: Synthesis, P&R, Bitstream
nodes.blurb: Your HDL flows through synthesis, place-and-route, and timing analysis before it becomes a bitstream loaded into the chip.

nodes.name: Sequential Logic & Flip-Flops
nodes.blurb: Registers built from flip-flops remember state between clock edges, turning combinational logic into machines with memory.

nodes.name: Fixed-Point vs Float
nodes.blurb: Fixed-point arithmetic is far cheaper and faster in fabric than IEEE floating point, at the cost of managing range and precision.

problems.name: Round and saturate a fixed-point accumulator

nodes.name: Blocking vs Non-Blocking Assignment
nodes.blurb: Use non-blocking for sequential logic and blocking for combinational, or your simulation and silicon will disagree.

problems.name: Fix a race from misused blocking assignment

nodes.name: Clocks, Reset & Fmax
nodes.blurb: The clock paces every register and Fmax is its highest workable frequency; resets can be synchronous or asynchronous, each with trade-offs.

problems.name: Estimate Fmax from a logic depth

nodes.name: BRAM vs Registers
nodes.blurb: Choose block RAM for dense addressed storage and registers for wide parallel access; the wrong choice wastes fabric or timing.

problems.name: Decide register file vs BRAM for a lookup

nodes.name: FSMs & One-Hot Encoding
nodes.blurb: Finite state machines sequence control logic; one-hot encoding trades flip-flops for shallow, fast decode logic.

nodes.name: Setup/Hold, Critical Path & Timing Closure
nodes.blurb: Data must be stable a setup time before and a hold time after each edge; the slowest register-to-register critical path sets your Fmax and must meet timing.

problems.name: Compute slack for a register-to-register path
Shorten a critical path by re-timing

nodes.name: Testbenches & Simulation
nodes.blurb: Simulation with a testbench drives stimulus and checks responses so you catch bugs before place-and-route.

nodes.name: Metastability & Clock-Domain Crossing
nodes.blurb: A signal sampled between clock domains can go metastable; a 2-FF synchronizer or async FIFO tames it.

nodes.name: Pipelining & Parallelism
nodes.blurb: Registering stages raises Fmax and throughput at the cost of latency and initiation interval; unrolling and replication process many items per cycle.

problems.name: Compute II of a pipelined loop
Replicate a comparator tree for wide search

nodes.name: HLS: C++ to RTL
nodes.blurb: High-level synthesis compiles annotated C++ into RTL, letting you pipeline and unroll with pragmas instead of hand-written HDL.

problems.name: Pipeline a loop with an HLS pragma
Partition an array for parallel access
Hit II=1 on a streaming kernel

nodes.name: Tick-to-Trade in Nanoseconds
nodes.blurb: HFT judges an FPGA on tick-to-trade latency and determinism: fixed, jitter-free nanoseconds from market data in to order out.

nodes.name: Inline-on-NIC Feed Handlers
nodes.blurb: A feed handler decodes market data directly in the NIC fabric, parsing packets on the wire with zero host round-trip.

nodes.name: Hardware Pre-Trade Risk
nodes.blurb: Risk checks run inline in fabric so every outgoing order is validated in a few deterministic nanoseconds before it leaves.

nodes.name: A/B Feed Arbitration
nodes.blurb: Exchanges publish redundant A and B feeds; hardware arbitrates them by sequence number to fill gaps at line rate.

problems.name: Arbitrate A/B feeds into one ordered stream

nodes.name: Kernel Bypass vs Full Hardware
nodes.blurb: Kernel bypass keeps strategy on the host CPU; full-hardware puts the whole tick-to-trade loop in fabric for the lowest latency.

problems.name: Compare latency: kernel bypass vs inline FPGA
Partition a strategy across host and fabric

nodes.name: CPU Cache & Cache Lines
nodes.blurb: The cache line, not the byte, is the real unit of memory access.

problems.name: Print the L1 cache line size
Pack a struct into one 64-byte cache line

nodes.name: Branch Prediction & Branchless Code
nodes.blurb: A mispredicted branch costs more than the work it guards, so remove it.

problems.name: Sort a branch-heavy loop to make it predictable

nodes.name: Bit Tricks & Integer Hacks
nodes.blurb: Bit manipulation replaces loops, branches, and division on the hot path.

problems.name: Round up to power of two
Check if a number is a power of two

nodes.name: Memory Hierarchy & Prefetching
nodes.blurb: Registers to DRAM span three orders of magnitude; prefetch to hide the miss.

problems.name: Measure the latency of each cache level

nodes.name: False Sharing & alignas
nodes.blurb: Two hot variables on one cache line ping-pong between cores for no reason.

nodes.name: Cache-Friendly Data Structures (SoA, flat maps)
nodes.blurb: Contiguous, flat layouts beat pointer-chasing trees on real hardware.

nodes.name: Type Punning & Strict Aliasing
nodes.blurb: Reinterpreting bits is essential yet trivially undefined if done wrong.

problems.name: Fast inverse square root bit hack

nodes.name: Hot-Path C++ (no virtual / exceptions / shared_ptr)
nodes.blurb: Virtual dispatch, throwing, and refcount bumps are latency you cannot afford.

problems.name: Replace shared_ptr with a raw handle
Return an error code instead of throwing

nodes.name: Denormals & FTZ
nodes.blurb: Subnormal floats trap into microcode; flush-to-zero keeps the pipeline fast.

problems.name: Benchmark a loop that produces denormals

nodes.name: The C++ Memory Model & memory_order
nodes.blurb: Acquire/release fences the compiler and CPU from reordering your loads and stores.

problems.name: Spot the data race in a relaxed load

nodes.name: Zero-Copy: RVO & noexcept
nodes.blurb: Let the compiler elide copies and moves so returning by value stays free.

problems.name: Force NRVO on a large return
Return a value instead of an out-param

nodes.name: Object Pools & No Hot-Path Allocation
nodes.blurb: malloc is unbounded latency; pre-allocate and recycle from a fixed pool.

nodes.name: Huge Pages & the TLB
nodes.blurb: 2MB pages shrink the page table so hot working sets stay TLB-resident.

problems.name: Back an allocation with 2MB huge pages

nodes.name: NUMA & Page Faults
nodes.blurb: Remote memory and first-touch faults add latency you must design away.

problems.name: Pin a thread to a NUMA node
Pre-fault a buffer to avoid hot-path page faults

nodes.name: Measuring Latency: rdtsc & Tail Percentiles
nodes.blurb: The mean lies; measure with the TSC and report p99 / p99.9 tails.

problems.name: Warm the instruction cache before timing

nodes.name: std::atomic
nodes.blurb: Lock-free primitives built on hardware atomics and the right memory order.

nodes.name: Kernel Bypass
nodes.blurb: Skip the syscall and the network stack; busy-poll the NIC from user space.

problems.name: Busy-poll a ring instead of blocking read
Sketch a DPDK-style RX poll loop

nodes.name: CAS & the ABA Problem
nodes.blurb: Compare-and-swap is the atom of lock-free code, and ABA is its classic trap.

nodes.name: Lock-Free vs Wait-Free
nodes.blurb: Lock-free guarantees system progress; wait-free guarantees it per thread.

nodes.name: SPSC Ring Buffer
nodes.blurb: The workhorse queue: single producer, single consumer, no locks, no allocation.

nodes.name: Syntax & Types
nodes.blurb: Python's core semantics: dynamic typing, truthiness, operators, and the runtime errors that catch newcomers off guard.

nodes.name: Lists, Dicts & Sets
nodes.blurb: The built-in workhorses: mutability, hashability, aliasing vs copying, and when to reach for a list, dict, set, or tuple.

nodes.name: Comprehensions & Slicing
nodes.blurb: Transform and reshape data in one expressive line: list/dict/set comprehensions, slice tricks, zip, and unpacking.

nodes.name: Functions & Closures
nodes.blurb: How Python binds names: default-argument evaluation, closures and late binding, scope rules (LEGB), and lambdas.

nodes.name: Strings
nodes.blurb: Immutable text done right: string methods return new objects, f-strings format anything, and interning is an implementation detail.

nodes.name: Iterators & Generators
nodes.blurb: Lazy evaluation with the iterator protocol: generators that compute on demand, single-pass exhaustion, and yield semantics.

nodes.name: The collections Module
nodes.blurb: Counter, defaultdict, and deque replace hand-rolled counting, grouping, and queue code with one import.

nodes.name: itertools & functools
nodes.blurb: The functional toolkit: accumulate running totals, memoize with lru_cache, and compose iterator pipelines without loops.

nodes.name: Sorting Idioms
nodes.blurb: sorted() with key functions: computed keys, stable tie-breaking, reverse pitfalls, and natural ordering with tuple keys.

nodes.name: Errors & Exceptions
nodes.blurb: EAFP over LBYL: precise except clauses, try/else/finally flow, and context managers that guarantee cleanup.

nodes.name: Arrays & Hashing
nodes.blurb: Dicts and sets give O(1) membership and counting — the Pythonic foundation for most interview array problems.

nodes.name: Two Pointers & Sliding Window
nodes.blurb: Converging indices and elastic windows in idiomatic Python — while loops over sorted data instead of nested scans.

problems.name: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted

nodes.name: Stacks
nodes.blurb: A plain list is Python's stack: append/pop for matching brackets, and monotonic stacks for next-greater scans.

nodes.name: Code Review: Spot the Bug
nodes.blurb: Read real broken Python and fix it: off-by-ones, aliasing leaks, late-binding closures, float traps, and swallowed exceptions.

problems.name: fix: Defaults drift after every request
fix: Every currency converts at the yen rate
fix: Ten dimes don't make a dollar

nodes.name: Trees & Graphs
nodes.blurb: Recursion for DFS, deque for BFS: traverse binary trees, flood-fill grids, and order dependencies topologically.

nodes.name: DP & Backtracking
nodes.blurb: Memoize recursion with lru_cache or tabulate bottom-up; backtrack by choosing, recursing, and un-choosing.

problems.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

nodes.name: Binary Search & Heaps
nodes.blurb: bisect the sorted (or the answer space) and let heapq keep the top-K: logarithmic tools for selection and search.

problems.name: Kth Largest Element in an Array
